hero.subtitle: How this boilerplate runs unit tests, enforces a global coverage baseline, and blocks commits that break the suite or drop below the threshold.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Every project built on this boilerplate ships with Jest as the test runner. Specs live next to the code as *.spec.ts files. A global coverage gate (branches, functions, lines, and statements) is defined in Jest config, and a Git pre-commit hook re-runs the full suite with coverage so regressions never land unnoticed.

points.title: What you get out of the box
items: Unit tests with Jest + ts-jest in a Node environment
Global coverage threshold of 80% for branches, functions, lines, and statements
npm scripts to run tests once, in watch mode, or with an HTML coverage report
Husky pre-commit that fails the commit when tests fail or coverage drops below the baseline

running.title: Running tests
running.description: From the project root, use the npm scripts defined in package.json:

items.desc: Run the full unit test suite once (no coverage report).
Re-run tests as files change — ideal while developing.
Run all tests and collect coverage. Also enforces the global threshold.

filter.title: Focus on a folder or pattern
filter.description: Pass Jest CLI flags after -- to narrow the run:

coverage.title: Coverage baseline
coverage.description: Coverage is configured in jest.config.js. The global threshold is the quality bar for every repository that uses this boilerplate:

thresholds.title: Global thresholds
items: branches: 80%
functions: 80%
lines: 80%
statements: 80%

collect.title: What is measured
collect.description: Only paths listed under collectCoverageFrom count toward the report. Spec files and interface-only files are typically excluded. When you add a new module with tests, include its source globs in that list so coverage stays honest.
items: Include production TypeScript under the areas you test
Exclude **/*.spec.ts
Exclude pure type/interface files when they have no runtime logic

viewing.title: Reading the coverage report
viewing.description: After npm run test:cov:
items: A table is printed in the terminal (per-file % for statements, branches, functions, lines)
An HTML report is written under coverage/lcov-report/index.html — open it in a browser for drill-down
The coverage/ directory is gitignored; regenerate it locally whenever you need the report

fail.title: When coverage fails
fail.description: If any global metric falls below 80%, Jest exits with a non-zero status. Fix by adding or strengthening unit tests for uncovered branches and paths — do not lower the threshold without a deliberate team decision.

precommit.title: Pre-commit hook
precommit.description: This boilerplate uses Husky so every commit runs the same gate as CI-minded local checks:

flow.title: What happens on commit
items: Git triggers .husky/pre-commit
The hook runs npm run test:cov
If any test fails or coverage is under 80%, the commit is aborted
On success, the commit proceeds as usual

setup.title: Setup after clone
setup.description: Hooks are installed via the prepare script (husky). Running npm install after cloning is enough for most environments.
items: Ensure Git is available and you are inside a clone of the repository
Run npm install so Husky can set core.hooksPath
Confirm .husky/pre-commit exists and is executable

skip.title: Emergency skip
skip.description: Skipping hooks with --no-verify bypasses the quality gate. Use it only in exceptional situations and follow up with a green npm run test:cov as soon as possible.

conventions.title: Conventions
items: Name specs *.spec.ts and keep them next to the unit under test
Prefer focused unit tests with mocked collaborators over slow end-to-end suites for domain logic
Match existing module patterns (controller / service / DTO / repository specs) when extending the codebase
Treat the 80% global threshold as a floor — new code should not regress the suite

checklist.title: Quick checklist
items: New feature or bugfix includes unit tests
npm test is green locally
npm run test:cov meets or exceeds 80% on all four metrics
New source areas are listed in collectCoverageFrom when they have specs
Commit succeeds without skipping the pre-commit hook
